sentence_reviews.text: Reproducibility and continuous monitoring track versions, seeds, data, metrics, drift, incidents, and operational health across experiments and deployments.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: NIST AI RMF covers documentation, monitoring, drift/change management, incidents, and measurement; DDIA covers data-system versioning, logs, replication, operations, and monitoring concepts; Deep Learning/AIMA support experiment/evaluation context.
sentence_reviews.notes: The cited sources support the main technical relationship, but the wording combines atlas taxonomy terms rather than a single quoted source claim.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Reproducibility, drift & continuous monitoring is to connect Checkpointing, recovery & experiment state, Benchmarks, test suites & task metrics to downstream capabilities including Evaluation-driven development & release gates, Observability, tracing & evaluation harnesses, Model registry, versioning & MLOps / LLMOps.
